paper_title: Antifeedant activity of essential oil Lantana camara L. against Spodoptera litura Fabr. (Lepidoptera: Noctuidae) and Plutella xylostella Curtis (Lepidoptera: Plutellidae)

entities.id: Antifeedant activity of essential oil Lantana camara L.
entities.description: The study investigating the deterrent effects of Lantana camara essential oil on insect pests.
Natural plant-derived products used for pest management in sustainable agriculture.
An ecosystem-based strategy that focuses on long-term prevention of pests.
The primary field of research for this study.

Process used to obtain essential oils using a Clevenger type apparatus.
Experiment method used to study the antifeedant activity where larvae have only treated food.
Experiment method where larvae can choose between treated and untreated food.
The primary material tested for antifeedant activity.
Concentration of the main volatile component identified in the oil.
Concentration of a specific volatile component in the oil.
